# The tau-amyloid synergy adds DAM cells rather than advancing them {#sec-trajectory}

```{r}
#| label: trajectory-setup
#| include: false
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(ggplot2)
})
options(warn = 2)                                    # promote any chunk warning to a render error (gate-critical)
targets::tar_source()                                # constants, plot theme + genotype scales
targets::tar_load(trajectory_figures)                # compact target only (no 612MB Seurat)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
theme_set(theme_tau())

trf   <- trajectory_figures
tr_pt <- trf$pt_density
tr_pt$genotype <- factor(as.character(tr_pt$genotype), levels = genotype_levels)
tr_pt$substate <- factor(as.character(tr_pt$substate),
                         levels = intersect(microglia_substate_levels, as.character(unique(tr_pt$substate))))
```

## Amyloid drives a large shift along the activation axis

```{r}
#| label: fig-trajectory-pt-density
#| fig-width: 9.6
#| fig-height: 5.6
#| fig-cap: "High-pseudotime density is concentrated in DAM states."
#| fig-alt: "Faceted area plots show high pseudotime concentrated in DAM states."
ggplot(tr_pt, aes(x_mid, n, fill = substate)) +
  geom_area(alpha = 0.78, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.15) +
  facet_grid(substate ~ genotype, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_substate(guide = "none") +
  labs(x = "activation pseudotime", y = "nuclei per bin",
       title = "Pseudotime density by genotype and substate") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
```
